title: diplora durvillei
intro: Diplora durvillei, also known as the "Milk Flower" or "Coral Milk Flower", is a cactus-like succulent native to South America, specifically in the regions of Argentina and Bolivia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Diplora durvillei include its ability to improve skin health, reduce inflammation, and even help with acne and other dermatological conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's extract is often used in creams, ointments, and lotions to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also available in capsule or tincture form for internal use.
